data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-13T01:04:12.703Z</current_datetime>

My xUnit.net project uses xunit 2.9.3 with xunit.runner.visualstudio on VSTest.
I want to switch to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. What should I do?

input.initialPrompt:
My xUnit.net project uses xunit 2.9.3 with xunit.runner.visualstudio on VSTest.
I want to switch to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. What should I do?

data.content:
Here's how to migrate your xUnit.net project to Microsoft.Testing.Platform:

**1. Update packages** in your `.csproj`:

```xml
<!-- Remove -->
<PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="..." />

<!-- Add -->
<PackageReference Include="xunit.v3.runner.mtp" Version="1.*" />
```

> **Note:** MTP requires **xUnit.net v3**. You'll also need to replace `xunit` (v2) with `xunit.v3`:
> ```xml
> <!-- Remove -->
> <PackageReference Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
> <!-- Add -->
> <PackageReference Include="xunit.v3" Version="1.*" />
> ```

**2. Enable MTP** in your `.csproj` properties:

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <TestingPlatformDotNetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotNetTestSupport>
</PropertyGroup>
```

**3. Fix breaking changes** (xUnit v2 → v3):
- Namespace: `Xunit` → `Xunit` (mostly the same, but some types moved)
- `Assert.Equal` on collections may behave differently
- `[Fact]` and `[Theory]` work the same
- `IClassFixture<T>` / `ICollectionFixture<T>` unchanged

**4. Run tests:**

```bash
dotnet test          # works with TestingPlatformDotNetTestSupport
dotnet run           # MTP also makes the test project a standalone executable
```

**Key benefits of MTP:** Your test project becomes a standalone executable, faster startup, extensible, and better CI integration.

See the [xUnit.net v3 migration guide](https://xunit.net/docs/getting-started/v3/migration) for a full list of API changes from v2 to v3.
data.reasoningText: The user wants to migrate their xUnit.net project from VSTest (using `xunit.runner.visualstudio`) to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP). Let me provide a concise guide.
